summary: Desktop design-to-code is ready for controlled batch generation with no hard blockers.

checks.evidence: snapshot_layout(problemsOnly=true) returned `No layout problems.` on 13 top-level product boards (excluding design-system showcase board).
10 `Implementation Contract / *` blocks standardized with canonical fields: route/layout/component-map/api/data-shape/state/permission/event/error-handling/analytics.
9 state-model sections aligned to unified flows; subtitle nodes include `stateFlow:` and `actionFlow:` (UGXQ7, G3kgo, nyqhl, 8Of0a, uSz6p, FqiUV, Pl7t2, D9tgD, ABpBW).
Design-system showcase board `MzSDs` is tagged `doc_showcase_board_exclude_from_codegen_scan`; known anomalies tagged `doc_only_not_for_codegen`.
Allowlist has 81 components; mapping manifest has 81 entries and 0 unknown/custom mappings.
No remaining placeholder flags detected in checked top-level trees.
Semantic token system is in place; however automated checks return resolved color values and cannot fully distinguish literal overrides from token bindings without code-side audit.

risks: Token-binding strictness still needs code-side verification during first generated page.
Design-system showcase board contains intentional clipped demo variants and must remain excluded from codegen scanning.
recommended_next_step: Step 6 pilot generation on one page variant (Auth or Dashboard), then validate compile/runtime + visual parity before full batch rollout.
